name: FELCO F 2 Classic Manual Hand Pruner

specs.Handle: Aluminium alloy with hard rubber grip
specs.Replaceability: All components available: blade, spring, bolt, grip, bumper
specs.Warranty: FELCO guarantees the product for life with proper care

whatWorks: The chrome-vanadium steel blade is sharper out of the box and holds its edge longer than any mass-market competitor — professional gardeners and orchardists consistently cite the blade quality as categorically different from hardware-store alternatives.

Every single component — blade, counter-blade, spring, bolt, handle grips, shock-absorbing bumper — is individually sold by FELCO and available through authorized dealers in Canada. This is a tool you service, not replace.
The wire-cutting notch on the lower blade handles garden ties, twist ties, and light wire without needing a separate tool. Small detail, genuinely useful during long pruning sessions.

Many professional gardeners, arborists, and orchardists have used the same pair of FELCO F2s for 10–20+ years. The combination of blade replaceability and Swiss build quality produces a tool with a genuinely measurable multi-decade lifespan.
worthKnowing: At $69 CAD, the F2 is expensive for hand pruners — entry-level alternatives run $15–25 CAD. The value case requires a 5–10 year planning horizon, not a one-season view.
Designed for right-handed users. The grip shape, blade geometry, and opening angle are optimised for right-hand use. Left-handed gardeners should look at the FELCO F 9.
The base model grip is hard rubber — not cushioned or padded. For users with hand fatigue or arthritis, FELCO's cushioned grip models (F 6, F 7, F 8) address this directly.
Requires occasional maintenance: blade sharpening every season or two (FELCO sells a sharpening tool), light oiling at the pivot point, and spring replacement every few years. The tool rewards care.

commonComplaints.title: Expensive for what looks like a…
commonComplaints.body: Expensive for what looks like a basic pruner. You have to commit to using it for 10+ years for the economics to make sense. Worth it if you prune regularly.

commonComplaints.title: Not designed for left-handed use…
commonComplaints.body: Not designed for left-handed use — I'm left-handed and found the blade angle and grip awkward. Should have bought the F9.

commonComplaints.title: Hard rubber grip with no cushioning…
commonComplaints.body: Hard rubber grip with no cushioning — after an hour of heavy pruning my hand was sore. FELCO sells cushioned versions for arthritis sufferers.

commonComplaints.title: Needs sharpening periodically unlike cheap…
commonComplaints.body: Needs sharpening periodically unlike cheap pruners you just throw away. Not a dealbreaker — the sharpening tool is $20 — but it's maintenance you have to plan for.

verdict: The FELCO F2 is the correct hand pruner for gardeners who prune more than occasionally — the Swiss blade quality, full parts replaceability, and proven multi-decade lifespan justify the $69 CAD price over any planning horizon longer than a few seasons. The base model is right-handed only and has no cushioning, which are real considerations for left-handed users or those with grip fatigue. For left-handed users, the FELCO F9. For cushioned grip, the FELCO F6 or F8. The F2 remains the industry standard for a reason.

realBuyers.quote: Same pair for twenty years. Resharpened the blade three times and replaced the spring once. Still cuts like new.
The difference between these and my old $20 pruners was immediately obvious. Like comparing a kitchen knife to a butter knife.
I'm a professional landscaper and FELCO F2 is what everyone I know uses. There's a reason for that.
Replacement blade is available at my local garden centre. No hunting online — just walked in and bought one.

sources.title: FELCO F2 Review — Gardening Know How

metaDescription: FELCO F2 hand pruner review for Canada: Swiss-made chrome-vanadium blade, full parts replaceability, 20+ year lifespan. Honest on right-hand-only design and hard grip. ~$69 CAD.
introText: The FELCO F2 is a Swiss-made bypass hand pruner with a fully replaceable hardened blade — the tool of choice for professional gardeners who measure tool lifespan in decades, not seasons. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B00002N64Y) at around $69 CAD.
